q: Within a plant, ___ and organic compounds are transported by being dissolved in water.
The dissolved mineral ions are transported in the ___ tissue.
The dissolved organic compounds are transported in the ___ tissue.
The uptake of water is a ___ process and occurs by osmosis.
Osmosis is the diffusion of water from a higher water potential to a ___ water potential.
In ___ absorption, the water first enters the cell sap and passes from one cell to another.
Movement where protoplasm is involved is called ___.
In passive absorption, water moves through the ___ of the root.
The apoplast path includes the cell wall and ___ spaces.
Most water travels via the ___ pathway when transpiration rates are high.
In the apoplastic pathway, water moves by ___ as it does not pass through a partially permeable membrane.
When water reaches the endodermis, the presence of a thick, waterproof, waxy band of ___ blocks the apoplastic pathway.
The band of suberin is called the ___ and forms an impassable barrier for the water.
The movement of water in the ___ pathway is slower than the apoplastic pathway.
In the symplastic pathway, water moves by ___ into the cell.
In the symplastic pathway, water moves between cells through the ___.
